data.title: D3XAT Faces Potential Attack with Estimated Losses of $160,000
data.description: According to PANews, Web3 security firm BlockSec has detected a suspected attack on D3XAT within the BSC ecosystem, with estimated losses reaching $160,000. Although the contract is not open-source, initial analysis suggests that the incident may involve price manipulation due to spot price dependency.

data.title: U.S. Economic Data Sparks Mixed Reactions Amid Fed Rate Cut Speculations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent U.S. economic data has led to fluctuating expectations regarding potential interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. Over the past week, retail sales in the U.S. showed broad growth, driven by automotive sales and major online promotions. However, consumer confidence unexpectedly declined for the first time since April, alongside rising inflation expectations.

Earlier reports on July's Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) presented mixed signals about the economy's resilience against U.S. President Donald Trump's comprehensive import tariffs. Both reports indicated persistent service inflation, with the PPI reaching a three-year high month-on-month. Within the Federal Reserve, there is notable disagreement over the possibility of a rate cut in September.

Key events this week include a speech by Federal Reserve Governor Waller at a blockchain seminar in Wyoming on Wednesday at 23:00 UTC+8. On Thursday, the Federal Reserve will release the minutes of its monetary policy meeting at 2:00 UTC+8, followed by a speech on economic prospects by 2027 FOMC voting member and Atlanta Fed President Bostic at 3:00 UTC+8. Additional data releases include initial jobless claims and the Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index for the week ending August 16, scheduled for Thursday at 20:30 UTC+8, and the preliminary August S&P Global Manufacturing and Services PMI at 21:45 UTC+8.

Federal Reserve Chair Powell is set to speak at the Jackson Hole Global Central Bank Annual Meeting on Friday at 22:00 UTC+8.

data.title: U.S. PPI Data Influences Bitcoin Market Dynamics
data.description:
According to PANews, a recent report by QCP highlights the impact of the U.S. Producer Price Index (PPI) data on interest rate expectations, effectively eliminating the possibility of a 50 basis point rate cut. In the cryptocurrency sector, U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent announced that the government will not sell confiscated Bitcoin and is exploring budget-neutral methods to acquire more Bitcoin to expand reserves.

Bitcoin's price, which fell from a high of $124,000 to $117,000, has stabilized around $119,000. The risk reversal is notably skewed towards bearish options. Despite macroeconomic pressures, QCP maintains that the upward trend in cryptocurrencies, which began in April this year, remains intact. This trend is supported by factors such as payment integration, balance sheet allocation, speculative positions, and increasing corporate adoption.

data.title: UAE Airlines Embrace Cryptocurrency for Ticket Purchases
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, travelers in the United Arab Emirates can now use cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin, Ethereum, and USDT to book flights with airlines including Emirates, Travala, and Air Arabia. This development marks a significant step in integrating digital currencies into mainstream travel services, offering passengers more flexible payment options.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/28380474254618?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows and Outflows
data.description:
According to PANews, data from SoSoValue indicates that on August 15, 2025, Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced a total net outflow of $14. 1295 million. The Blackrock ETF IBIT recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $114 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $58. 674 billion
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/28378327501193?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: White House Ranks U.S. Companies on Support for 'Big and Beautiful' Act
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a report from The Daily Telegraph reveals that U.S. President Donald Trump's aides have developed a scoring system to rank 553 American companies and industry associations based on their support for the 'Big and Beautiful' Act. A White House official stated that the list evaluates the efforts made by these companies to support and promote the act. Factors influencing the scores include social media posts, press releases, video endorsements, advertisements, participation in White House events, and activities related to the act.

The list categorizes companies as 'high,' 'medium,' or 'low' partners in relation to the act. Axios, a U.S. news outlet, disclosed that the list highlights 'exemplary partners,' including Uber, DoorDash, United Airlines, Delta Air Lines, the American Airlines Association, AT&T, Cisco, and the American Iron and Steel Institute. These companies have taken steps to advocate for Trump's 'Big and Beautiful' Act.

data.title: Ethereum Stablecoin Supply Sees Significant Increase
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Artemis indicates that the supply of stablecoins on the Ethereum network has surged by $16.4 billion over the past month. This substantial growth reflects the increasing demand and utilization of stablecoins within the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows and Outflows
data.description: According to PANews, data from SoSoValue indicates that on August 15, 2025, Bitcoin spot ETFs experienced a total net outflow of $14.1295 million. The Blackrock ETF IBIT recorded the highest single-day net inflow of $114 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $58.674 billion. Conversely, the Grayscale ETF GBTC saw the largest single-day net outflow of $81.8171 million, with its historical total net outflow reaching $23.803 billion. As of the latest update, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs stands at $151.979 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 6.54% relative to Bitcoin's total market capitalization. The cumulative historical net inflow for Bitcoin spot ETFs has reached $54.974 billion.

data.title: Ukrainian President Zelensky to Visit Oval Office
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump announced that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is scheduled to visit the Oval Office on Monday afternoon.
